sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.30 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: (Well-known question). A discrete group$G$is said to have the (also known as Gromov's a-T-menability property) if there exists a metrically proper isometric action of$G$on a (possibly infinite-dimensional) Hilbert space. Are all 1-relator groups Haagerup groups? {{ J.\,O.\,Button}}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
